basics.name: Ramu Kamath
basics.label: Senior Data Platform Engineer | Data Architect
basics.summary: Results-driven Data Platform Engineer with a strong background in designing, automating, and optimizing modern data infrastructure for large-scale analytics and processing. Passionate about building resilient platforms that balance innovation with environmental and cost-conscious responsibility.

work.name: ABN AMRO Bank N.V.
highlights: Designed and deployed scalable Databricks lakehouse architecture on Azure for the bank's next-gen Data & AI platform
Automated infrastructure provisioning on Azure using Terraform and Helm-based deployment on AKS
Implemented Unity Catalog setup and feature development for bank-wide data governance
Implemented CI/CD best practices for data engineering using Databricks Asset Bundles (DAB)
Set up modern ingestion and ETL way of working for domain data teams
Templating using Cookiecutter for automating the creation of repositories
Working with sustainability team on FinOps to optimize cloud resource usage and reduce cost
Contributor in the data engineering community, sharing best practices and mentoring peers
Responsible for building and maintaining a reliable and scalable orchestration framework using Airflow (AKS)

work.name: ABN AMRO Bank
highlights: Designed and implemented data pipeline architecture for Domain Data Store
Designed and implemented Data Quality Framework for proactive monitoring and reporting of DQ issues
Built command line utility to automate generation of ETL configuration, reducing development time and manual errors
Guided the data engineering community on best practices and solution design
Reduced processing of historical data through parallel data processing architecture
Worked with Databricks to optimize product usage and reduce cost

work.name: NN Investment Partners
highlights: Designed and built end-to-end application stack on AWS
Created ETL historical load engine to process data into application
Guided the team on data engineering best practices
Built reference database using graph database (Neptune)
Automated infrastructure using CDK (TypeScript) and CloudFormation
Set up and authored/orchestrated data pipelines using Airflow

work.name: Datlinq - a Roamler Company
highlights: Designed and delivered scalable, cost-efficient data pipelines for real-time and batch workloads
Set up a containerized orchestration environment with automated DAGs for data workflows
Architected and implemented a unified, centralized data store to improve data accessibility
Led migration of data infrastructure from GCP to AWS, achieving a 30% cost reduction
Enabled automated recommendation services through efficient pipeline design
Built and optimized real-time data streaming solutions for business-critical applications

work.name: Aegon Nederland
highlights: Designed and built data pipelines using AWS Glue to ingest data into the finance publication layer
Set up a data lake for financial data sources
Created metadata-driven data ingestion and processing
Set up CI/CD pipeline and infrastructure services using CloudFormation and Ansible

work.name: Accenture
highlights: Worked with a data lake environment and ingested numerous data sources (modem logs, CDRs, commercial data, weblogs) into HDFS
Planned and executed a semantic layer to expose data to downstream applications
Created reusable and portable ETL solutions for data pipelines
Documented projects with version control and automated integration with CI/CD pipeline
Rationalised manual reporting services and built integrated data warehouse solutions
